title: Disabling 2FA
content:
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) adds a critical layer of security by requiring a one-time code from an authenticator app each time a user logs in. However, there are situations where 2FA must be temporarily or permanently disabled — most commonly when a user loses access to their authenticator device and cannot generate codes.

Common Reasons for Disabling 2FA

  • Lost device: The user's phone was lost, stolen, or factory-reset, and they no longer have access to their authenticator app.
  • App uninstalled: The authenticator app was accidentally deleted, taking the Tklesson account entry with it.
  • Recovery codes lost: The user cannot locate their backup recovery codes and has no other way to log in.
  • Account locked out: Multiple failed 2FA attempts have prevented the user from accessing their dashboard.

Account Security Verification

Before disabling 2FA for any user, you must verify their identity using the same security checks as a password reset:

  1. What is your full name?
  2. What is your personal phone number on file?
  3. What are the last four digits of a payment method used on our platform?

If no payment method exists on file, skip the third question. Never disable 2FA without completing these verification steps — doing so could allow an impersonator to gain full access to the account.

Completing the 2FA Disable

Once identity is confirmed:

  1. Navigate to User Lookup and search for the user by name or email.
  2. Click their email address to open User Management.
  3. Verify that the phone number and account status displayed match the information provided by the caller.
  4. Click Disable 2FA and confirm the action when prompted.

The system will immediately clear the user's 2FA settings — including their secret key and all backup codes. The user will now be able to log in with only their email and password.

Before Closing the Conversation

Once the user confirms they have regained access, strongly advise them to re-enable 2FA immediately. Walk them through the setup process if needed:

  • From their dashboard, navigate to Settings or Account Security.
  • Click Enable Two-Factor Authentication.
  • Scan the QR code with their authenticator app.
  • Enter the 6-digit verification code to confirm setup.
  • Save the recovery codes in a secure location — these are the backup if the device is lost again.

Remind the user that without 2FA, their account is protected only by their password, which is significantly less secure. Confirm they can log in successfully before ending the conversation.
